This is a minimal, reproducible demo extracted from [workbook.qmd](workbook.qmd) to generate the Seville interactive flow map.

```{r}
#| eval: false
pak::pak("tdscience/tartu26")
library(spanishoddata)
library(flowmapblue)
library(tidyverse)
library(sf)
```

```{r}
#| label: flowmap-seville-demo
#| eval: false
#| echo: true
#| code-summary: "Minimal example: interactive flow map for Seville"

# Get OD zones and recent dates
zones <- spod_get_zones(zones = "distr", ver = 2)
valid_dates <- spod_get_valid_dates(2)
recent_dates <- tail(valid_dates, 3)

# Identify Seville districts and define a functional urban area (10 km buffer)
zones_seville <- zones |>
  filter(grepl("^Sevilla distrito", name, ignore.case = TRUE))
zones_seville_fua <- zones[st_buffer(zones_seville, dist = 10000), ]

# Prepare centroid locations for flowmapblue
sf::sf_use_s2(FALSE)
locations_seville <- zones_seville_fua |>
  st_transform(crs = 4326) |>
  st_centroid() |>
  st_coordinates() |>
  as.data.frame() |>
  mutate(id = zones_seville_fua$id) |>
  rename(lon = X, lat = Y)

# NOTE: Ensure flows_seville_time is created before running this block.
flows_seville_time <- readRDS("flows_seville_time.rds") # Load pre-processed flows data
locations_seville <- readRDS("locations_seville.rds") # Load pre-processed locations data
flowmap_seville_interactive <- flowmapblue(
  locations = locations_seville,
  flows = flows_seville_time,
  mapboxAccessToken = Sys.getenv("MAPBOX_TOKEN"),
  darkMode = TRUE,
  animation = FALSE,
  clustering = TRUE
)

flowmap_seville_interactive
```

<details>
<summary>Getting a Mapbox token</summary>

Get a free token from https://account.mapbox.com/access-tokens/

``` r
Sys.setenv(MAPBOX_TOKEN = "YOUR_MAPBOX_ACCESS_TOKEN")
```
</details>
